description: There is a latent bug in `calculate_mrr()`: when a relevance vector `r` contains only `NA` values, `which(as.logical(r))[1L]` returns `NA` (not an empty integer), causing `is.na(first_hit)` to evaluate TRUE and correctly return 0 -- but `as.logical(NA)` produces `NA` not `FALSE`, which means `which()` treats NA elements as NA and skips them, still accidentally working. However, a vector of all-NA integers (e.g. `c(NA_integer_, NA_integer_)`) is NOT treated as all-FALSE, so the function silently produces NaN in some edge cases. Fix `calculate_mrr()` to coerce each element with `!is.na(r) & as.logical(r)` before passing to `which()`, ensuring NA relevance flags are treated as non-relevant.
